name: jengo/schema
description: Custom declarative ORM and Database Schema mapping tool for CodeIgniter 4. Enables data hydration, fluent query building, relationship mapping, and computed properties using PHP Attributes. Responses are non-paginated by default, and pagination is activated when limit is set outright. Pagination links automatically append the pagination group suffix (e.g. ?page_custom_group=X) if a custom group is specified.

attributes.description: Maps the annotated Schema class to an underlying CodeIgniter 4 Model and Entity.

arguments.entity: class-string (Optional Entity class for custom hydration)

attributes.description: Marks the target property as the primary key of the database schema.
Maps a property to a database field, enabling searchable filters and value casting.

arguments.searchable: bool (If true, this field is scanned during search operations)
arguments.cast: Jengo\Schema\Hydration\Enums\Cast (Casts database values to array, bool, int, float, string, object, datetime)

attributes.description: Annotates a getter method to declare a computed field dynamically generated based on dependent fields.

arguments.name: string (The name of the computed attribute in JSON output)
arguments.dependants: array of strings (Fields that must be selected/fetched for the computation to run)
arguments.cast: Jengo\Schema\Hydration\Enums\Cast (Optional cast for the computed result)

attributes.usage:
#[Computed(name: 'full_name', dependants: ['first_name', 'last_name'])]
public function getFullName(): string { return $this->first_name . ' ' . $this->last_name; }

attributes.description: Declares a derived field that fetches data dynamically outside standard table columns.

arguments.foreignKey: string (The local foreign key column mapping)
string (The target table's foreign key mapping)

query_builder.description: Fluent query wrapper constructed via the query() helper. Manages criteria composition, join resolution, sorting, and pagination. In OPEN mode, pagination (page and limit) is controlled directly via request parameters. Use paginationGroup() to set the group name and align request keys (e.g. ?page_group=X) without needing paginate() unless setting default fallback limits.

methods.where: where(string $column, mixed $value, bool $isOr = false): self (Applies basic filter)
methods.limit: limit(int $limit): self (Applies query limit and enables pagination)
methods.paginate: paginate(int $page, int $perPage = 15, string $group = 'default'): self (Applies page, limit, and configures pagination group for generating links)
methods.paginationGroup: paginationGroup(string $group): self (Sets custom pagination group used for links)
methods.search: search(?string $term, array $fields = [], string $side = 'both', ?bool $caseInsensitive = null): self (Performs an advanced search with custom columns, wildcard direction, and case-sensitivity control)
methods.inline: inline(): self (Runs in manual inline builder mode)
methods.open: open(): self (Runs in dynamic request-driven hydration mode, auto-building queries based on client request parameters)
methods.first: first(): QueryResult (Executes query and returns first matched record)
methods.find: find(mixed $id): QueryResult (Performs where clause on primary key and returns first match)
methods.get: get(): QueryResult (Executes and returns hydrated results)

helpers.name: query
helpers.description: Agnostic entry point to start a schema query chain.

helpers.name: dump_query
helpers.description: Dumps all queries executed by the QueryLogger for debugging purposes (disabled in production).
